title: arabis ochroleuca
intro: Arabis ochroleuca, also known as white mountain rockcress, is a perennial plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system. Arabis ochroleuca can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional medicine as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
